response.justification: While this candidate demonstrates exceptional breadth as a software engineer with 15+ years of experience, they lack the two mandatory pillars of this role: financial services domain expertise and current C++ proficiency on Windows platforms. Their background is predominantly in web development, backend services, and open-source tools (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang), with C++ experience relegated to an earlier role at NI and appearing dated. There is no evidence of SQL Server/Oracle knowledge, fixed income/derivatives experience, or capital markets domain exposure. The skill gaps are fundamental rather than marginal.
strengths: Exceeds experience requirement with 15+ years of professional software development and demonstrated mastery of software engineering principles (TDD, CI/CD, clean code practices)
Has Java development background and some Python exposure, both desirable for the role; strong DevOps and infrastructure knowledge
Excellent communication skills evidenced by public talks, workshops, and open-source contributions; proven ability to mentor and share knowledge
concerns: C++ experience is dated and not a recent primary focus; career trajectory shows migration away from C++ toward TypeScript/Node.js, indicating stale expertise in the mandatory core skill
Complete absence of capital markets, fixed income, derivatives, or investment banking domain knowledge; no evidence of financial services experience whatsoever
No demonstrated expertise with Windows platform development, SQL Server, or Oracle; recent work centers on Linux, Docker, cloud infrastructure, and web technologies

key_factors.factor: Dated C++ expertise, not current primary skill
Software engineering fundamentals and CI/CD knowledge
